{"problem_standardized_math":"Let \(A=[\,B\ N\,]\), where the basis matrix \(B\) consists of the first \(m\) columns of \(A\) (so that \(P=I\) in (15.7)), and define\n\[\nY=\begin{bmatrix}\nI\\\\\n(B^{-1}N)^T\n\end{bmatrix},\qquad\nZ=\begin{bmatrix}\n-B^{-1}N\\\\\nI\n\end{bmatrix}.\n\]\nShow that the particular solution \(Y(AY)^{-1}b\) determined by this choice of \(Y\) is the minimum-norm solution of \(Ax=b\). More specifically, show that\n\[\nY(AY)^{-1}=A^T(AA^T)^{-1}.\n\]"}
